location.office_time_required: up to 10% travel; visibility across global offices expected

level_signals: 12+ years in business operations, strategic operations, or Chief of Staff roles
Experience building and leading high-performing teams
Works directly with Co-CEOs and senior leadership team
Comp range $224K–$336K base consistent with senior director / VP level
'Head of' title implies functional ownership and people leadership

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 12+ years in business operations, strategic operations, or Chief of Staff roles at high-growth technology companies — ideally with experience across both hypergrowth and post-IPO phases
Proven track record of building and running a company-wide operating system — OKRs, planning, performance management — not just participating in one someone else designed
Experience leading through major organizational inflection points: rapid scaling, go-public events, integrations, or significant structural change
Demonstrated ability to work directly with and influence CEOs and senior leadership teams without relying on positional authority — through clarity, credibility, and quality of thinking
Experience building and leading high-performing teams; a developer of people, not just a driver of output
Financial fluency is non-negotiable: comfort with LRP/annual planning cycles, unit economics, and working alongside a CFO and finance org as a true peer — not just a consumer of their outputs
Exceptional communicator: writes with precision, speaks with confidence, and can hold a room with a Board narrative or an all-hands equally well
Moves with urgency: comfortable in short iterative cycles, makes decisions with incomplete information, creates momentum where others create decks
Operationally obsessed: you notice inefficiency like a splinter; you can't leave it alone until it's fixed

AI-native operator: you understand how to design and leverage AI systems to automate reporting, surface real-time visibility into KPIs and OKRs, and drive company-wide efficiency — and you're resourceful enough to go find the source data yourself rather than waiting for someone to hand it to you
Data-driven and influential: you don't just analyze — you use data as a lever; your insights shape decisions, change minds, and move the org toward better outcomes
A natural integrator: builds trust with functional leaders, navigates tension without drama, keeps the company moving without becoming the bottleneck

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Background in management consulting or investment banking a plus; operators who built their skills in the trenches are equally valued
SaaS, B2B software, or marketing technology experience preferred
ideally with experience across both hypergrowth and post-IPO phases

explicitly_excluded: This is not a chief of staff role that begins and ends with the CEO's calendar.
It is not a program manager who tracks work other people own.
It is not a strategic advisor who synthesizes without executing.

scope.surface: Company-wide operating architecture — strategy-setting, OKRs, KPIs, cross-functional execution, CEO and leadership team effectiveness, Board management
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / B2C CRM (autonomous B2C CRM platform)
primary_metrics_inferred: Leadership team and Board meeting effectiveness
core_responsibilities: Lead annual and multi-year operating planning process
Own company-wide OKR process end to end
Define and manage company KPIs; run performance reviews and operational audits
Drive automation of KPIs and OKR results using AI tools
Own the management operating system (meetings, cadences, decision forums)
Lead and/or support highest-priority cross-functional initiatives
Design and run operating cadences for Co-CEOs and senior leadership team
Support preparation of Board meeting materials and manage Board portal
Attend meetings on behalf of Co-CEOs as needed
Champion AI adoption across the company

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as part of total compensation package

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and wide ($224K–$336K base) with equity and bonus mentioned
Clear articulation of what the role is NOT — signals internal alignment on scope
Explicit people leadership expectation signals real team ownership, not solo operator
JD written with unusual specificity and voice — suggests genuine thought went into defining the role
Up to 10% travel only — relatively low for a role of this seniority
red_flags: Very broad scope ('the whole operating architecture') could mean competing priorities and diffuse ownership in practice
Dual Co-CEO reporting structure adds political complexity not explored in JD
12+ years minimum is a firm bar — limited flexibility implied by the seniority and financial fluency being called 'non-negotiable'
No visa sponsorship information provided — unclear for international applicants
Location type is ambiguous: JD mentions global offices and travel but no specific city requirement is stated

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, 12+ years requirement, and 'Head of' title)
seniority.manages_people (inferred from 'Experience building and leading high-performing teams')
scope.business_model (inferred from 'autonomous B2C CRM company' in JD body)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed)
location.type set to hybrid (JD references global offices, all-hands, and travel but no explicit remote/hybrid policy statement)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from US salary range covering 'various US geographic markets' with no city restriction)
ambiguities: Work location policy is never explicitly stated as remote, hybrid, or onsite. JD references showing up 'visibly across Klaviyo — at all-hands, in team meetings, in KRGs, in global offices' and 10% travel, suggesting some in-person presence but no formal hybrid/onsite mandate stated. Treated as hybrid.
Visa sponsorship: no mention in JD body — left as 'unclear'.
The '12+ years' requirement is listed under 'Experience' without a 'Required' label, but given the seniority and the 'non-negotiable' language used elsewhere (for financial fluency), treated as hard.
Hypergrowth and post-IPO experience is flagged as 'ideally' suggesting it is preferred, but is embedded within the primary experience bullet — classified as nice-to-have with soft severity.
